# Simploy

> A simple CLI tool for SSH-based deployment. Compress, upload, extract, run commands on remote servers.

## Architecture

```
src/
├── index.ts                    # CLI entry point (commander)
├── types.ts                    # All shared TypeScript interfaces
├── errors.ts                   # Custom error class hierarchy (SimployError > ConfigError, SSHError, DeployError, VariableError)
├── constants.ts                # Default config templates, file name constants, schema URL
├── commands/
│   ├── init.ts                 # `simploy init` - creates config files with $schema
│   └── deploy.ts               # `simploy deploy` - deploys to remote servers
├── core/
│   ├── config-loader.ts        # Loads + validates simploy.json (zod), loads simploy.secrets.json
│   ├── variable-resolver.ts    # Recursive ${VAR} substitution with circular reference detection + undefined variable errors
│   ├── ssh-manager.ts         # `withSSH()` wrapper - connects/disposes SSH with try/finally guarantee
│   ├── file-transfer.ts       # Compress local dir → upload zip → extract on remote → cleanup temp files
│   └── remote-shell.ts        # Execute remote shell commands sequentially, stop on first failure
└── utils/
    ├── logger.ts               # chalk-based logging helpers
    └── spinner.ts              # ora spinner wrapper
```

## CLI Commands

- `simploy init` — Creates `simploy.json` (with `$schema`) and `simploy.secrets.json` in cwd. Prompts before overwriting. Adds `simploy.secrets.json` to `.gitignore`.
- `simploy deploy` — Deploys project to remote servers.
  - `-e, --env <env>` — Environment name (default: `dev`)
  - `-s, --server <name>` — Deploy to a specific server only
  - `-c, --config-path <path>` — Config file path (default: `simploy.json`)
  - `-p, --secrets-path <path>` — Secrets config file path (default: `simploy.secrets.json`)
  - `--dry-run` — Preview deployment plan without executing

## `$schema` — Schema Discovery for AI Agents & IDEs

`simploy init` automatically adds `$schema` to `simploy.json`. The schema URL points to `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JunDev76/Simploy/main/dist/schema.json`.

AI agents discovering a `simploy.json` file can follow the `$schema` URL to understand:
- What Simploy is (SSH deployment tool)
- The full config structure and field meanings
- Required vs optional fields and validation rules

## Config Schema (simploy.json)

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JunDev76/Simploy/main/dist/schema.json",
  "environments": {
    "<env_name>": {
      "servers": [
        {
          "name": "server-name",
          "ssh": {
            "host": "${HOST_VAR}",
            "port": 22,
            "username": "${USER_VAR}",
            "password": "${PASS_VAR}",
            "privateKeyPath": "~/.ssh/id_rsa",
            "passphrase": ""
          },
          "localPath": ".",
          "remotePath": "/home/user/app",
          "exclude": ["node_modules", ".git"],
          "preserve": [".env"],
          "preDeploy": ["screen -S app -X quit"],
          "postDeploy": ["cd /home/user/app", "npm ci", "npm start"]
        }
      ],
      "variables": {
        "APP_NAME": "myapp"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

- `$schema`: Optional. JSON Schema URI for IDE autocomplete and AI agent discovery.
- `ssh`: Must have either `password` or `privateKeyPath`. `port` defaults to 22.
- `exclude`: Files/dirs excluded from the zip archive. `node_modules`, `.git`, `simploy.json`, `simploy.secrets.json` are always excluded.
- `preserve`: Files preserved during remote cleanup. If empty or omitted, `rm -rf remotePath/*` is used instead.
- `preDeploy`: Commands run before file transfer (e.g., stop services).
- `postDeploy`: Commands run after file transfer (e.g., install deps, start app).
- `variables`: Key-value pairs used for `${VAR}` substitution.

## simploy.secrets.json

Flat key-value object for secrets. Merged with `variables` for substitution. Auto-added to `.gitignore` by `simploy init`.

```json
{
  "DEV_WEB_HOST": "your.server.com",
  "DEV_WEB_USER": "ubuntu",
  "DEV_WEB_PASSWORD": "yourpassword"
}
```

## Variable Resolution

- Syntax: `${VAR_NAME}` (uppercase letters, digits, underscore)
- Recursive: Variables can reference other variables (`"HOST": "${IP}:${PORT}"`)
- Circular references throw `VariableError`
- Undefined variables throw `VariableError` (deployment aborts)
- Resolution order: environment `variables` first, then `simploy.secrets.json` overrides

## Error Hierarchy

```
SimployError (base)
├── ConfigError      — config file not found, invalid JSON, schema validation failure
├── SSHError         — connection failure, remote command failure
├── DeployError      — file transfer/extraction failure
└── VariableError    — undefined variable, circular reference
```

All `SimployError` subclasses produce user-friendly messages. Unexpected errors show stack traces only in `NODE_ENV=development`.

## Deployment Flow

1. Load & validate `simploy.json` (zod schema)
2. Load `simploy.secrets.json` (optional)
3. Merge variables
4. For each server in environment:
   a. Resolve all `${VAR}` in config values
   b. If `--dry-run`, print plan and skip
   c. Connect SSH (`withSSH` guarantees `dispose()`)
   d. Execute `preDeploy` commands
   e. Compress local dir → upload zip → clean remote → extract → remove temp zip
   f. Execute `postDeploy` commands
5. Print summary. Exit code 1 if any server failed.

## Tech Stack

- Node.js >=18, TypeScript, ESM
- commander (CLI), node-ssh (SSH), archiver (zip), zod (validation), chalk/ora (output)
- vitest (testing)
